executive_summary: The old campaign failed for a structural, not algorithmic, reason: it only ever ran Stage A (combat-only, bare 10-card starter deck sampled uniformly against the full Act-1 pool where 27% of fights are elites/bosses a starter deck can never win), hit a hard ceiling near 62%, and the unreachable 0.85 promotion gate + `if not promoted: break` (train_necrobinder.py:440) permanently halted the ladder before a single full-run gradient was ever taken. Compounding faults: mean-pooled hand encoding (policy.py:95) blinds the per-slot action head to which card is in which slot; the deck is invisible outside combat (7 aggregate scalars, rich_observation.py:499-524) so synergy-aware drafting is unlearnable; LR linearly decayed to ~1.4e-7 (frozen optimizer, not revived on resume); the reward function jittered ~40% every eval from a win-rate-driven shaping anneal and zeroed shaping above 80% win rate; truncation was scored identically to death; and the exact, cheap, resettable simulator — the project's decisive asset — was never used for search. The unified plan deletes stages A/B, trains ONE full-run agent under an ascension x act-count curriculum that never hard-halts, replaces all hackable shaping with provably-invariant potential-based shaping (PBRS), fixes the architecture blindness (per-slot concat + deck bag), keeps the optimizer alive (constant LR + target_kl, floored entropy), then layers cheap sample-efficiency accelerators (scripted-heuristic BC bootstrap, Self-Imitation Learning, an Act-4 state bank) and reserves simulator search (MCTS/ExIt offline + shallow local inference-time MCTS on elite/boss/Heart turns) as the Heart-specific escalation and the only realistic path toward the aspirational 95%. Inference stays fully local throughout (compact policy net; optional shallow local MCTS). Honest achievable A10 full-run-to-Heart win rate: ~55-70% with the model-free+imitation core, pushing toward ~70-80% with inference-time search; 95% is above top-human rates and is a stretch target, not a gate.
chosen_approach: Staged synthesis, not a single proposal. SPINE = Design A (pure model-free MaskablePPO on the full run, ascension x act-count curriculum, PBRS, architecture + optimizer fixes) because it is the lowest-risk thing that relaunches today and, critically, produces measurable per-act/Heart telemetry so every later decision is data-driven. LAYERED ACCELERATORS = the highest-ROI, lowest-code pieces of Design C: (1) a scripted-heuristic BC bootstrap to kill the cold-start, (2) Self-Imitation Learning to replay rare wins against the single sparse terminal, and (3) an Act-4 STATE BANK that snapshots the agent's own runs the moment they reach Act 4 and resets a fraction of envs directly into the Heart region — the cheapest possible fix for the Heart being reached only at the end of near-won runs. ESCALATION = Design B's simulator search, adopted deliberately and last, in two forms: offline MCTS/ExIt value+policy targets to densify deckbuilding credit if SIL+state-bank plateau, and a shallow (64-128 sim) local inference-time MCTS gated to elite/boss/Heart turns only — the single largest lever toward the final percentages and the only one that plausibly approaches 95%, while remaining fully local (STS2 is turn-based, latency is free). We start with scripted-heuristic BC rather than MCTS-BC because it needs no search infrastructure and de-risks immediately; the MCTS infra is built once and reused for both offline ExIt and inference. Architecture change is the minimum-viable per-slot CONCAT (not the full pointer head) first, with the pointer head as a measured stretch. This brackets risk: we get a strong, honest ~60% base fast, then spend search only where telemetry proves it is needed (the Heart).
concrete_implementation_plan: PHASE 0 (immediate, ~1 day): ship the phase1_minimal_changes below and relaunch a real full-run training run (G1: ascension 0, acts 1-2, existing shaping fixed to a constant scale, truncation decoupled from death, constant LR + target_kl, floored entropy, n_envs=24, n_steps=1024, gamma=0.997, ladder that never hard-halts). Goal: confirm the agent reaches Act 2 and win-rate climbs; establish the telemetry baseline (per-act breakdown now works because run-env info carries floor/act).

PHASE 1 (reward correctness): rewrite reward_config.py to potential-based shaping. Add potential(mgr) -> 0.45*progress + 0.30*effective_hp + 0.20*enemy_down (enemy_down in-combat only, carried between combats); emit F = gamma_shape*Phi(s') - Phi(s) every step in rich_run_env.step with Phi=0 at terminal. progress = (current_act_index + clip(act_floor/17,0,1))/4; effective_hp = (hp + 0.5*block + 0.3*osty_hp)/max_hp clipped [0,1]. Delete floor/act_completion/combat_hp_retention terms and the win-rate-driven shaping anneal entirely. Keep win=+1/death=-1, truncation=0.0 (bootstrap), sim_error=0.0 (tag info['sim_error']=True, do NOT score as death). PBRS is provably policy-invariant so it needs no anneal and cannot be farmed; keep a constant shaping weight.

PHASE 2 (observability): extend rich_observation.encode_run with a run-level DECK BAG (NUM_CARD_IDS counts of player.deck / BAG_COUNT_SCALE, projected through the SAME shared card_embedding table like the combat pile bags at rich_observation.py:336) plus ~8 Necrobinder archetype scalars from the deck (summon-card count, Soul-generator count, Soul-payoff count, Doom-applier count, ethereal count, OstyAttack count, zero-cost count, upgrade fraction). Bump RUN_SIZE/RICH_OBS_SIZE, add offset constants, update segment_table() and the assert in RichFeaturesExtractor. Validate with the rich_observation segment tests before any long run. This is the prerequisite for any synergy-aware drafting/removal.

PHASE 3 (architecture): in policy.py RichFeaturesExtractor.forward replace hand_pooled = hand_x.mean(dim=1) with a per-slot CONCAT (reshape (B,10,H) -> (B,10*H)) aligned to the per-slot combat action indices, plus a mean-pool appended as global context. Add the deck-bag projection and the 8 archetype scalars to the returned features. Bump card_embed_dim 64->96, hand_hidden 64->96. Recompute features_dim; update rich_policy_kwargs defaults and transfer_weights coverage. Smoke-test logits before launch. STRETCH (only if per-slot concat underperforms on multi-target cards): a pointer/attention combat action head keyed on per-slot embeddings for actions 0..114, standard linear head for meta actions 115..156.

PHASE 4 (curriculum ramp to A10 + 4 acts): drive the single full-run env up two axes via a CurriculumController callback that pushes ascension ceiling and max_act_count into all envs via env_method each eval and NEVER returns False. Stage table (advance on gate-hit x2 OR budget-exhaustion, warm-start each from prev best): G1 A0 acts1-2 20M gate0.80; G2 A0 acts1-4 30M gate0.55 (opens the Heart early so the state bank starts filling); G3 A4 acts1-4 40M gate0.50; G4 A8 acts1-4 50M gate0.45 (SIL on); G5 A10 acts1-4 60M final. Per-reset ascension sampler {ceiling:0.6, U(0..ceiling):0.3, min(ceiling+2,10):0.1} for anti-forgetting. ~200M total RL steps.

PHASE 5 (throughput, in parallel with Phase 4): n_envs 16->24; compute the action mask ONCE per env.step() and reuse it in action_masks()/info (kill the ~2x redundant compute; can_play_card is ~40% of step time), memoize can_play_card over (hand,energy,powers) within a turn; move to a shared-memory VecEnv or dirty-flag the static obs segments (deck/relic/run-static) in encode_run to cut the ~19KB/env/step IPC. Target ~3-3.5k end-to-end steps/s so 200M steps lands in ~17-20h. Re-benchmark and re-scale the budget (drop to ~120M) if the target is missed rather than assuming.

PHASE 6 (imitation bootstrap, offline): write scripts/heuristic_agent.py (rule-based Necrobinder: block if incoming>=HP else best on-Osty attack, summon if Osty dead and a summon is in hand; drafting prefers summon/Soul/Doom density and removes Strikes; simple rest/shop/map heuristics) and scripts/gen_bc_data.py (N worker processes play WHOLE runs, return batched trajectories, no per-step IPC -> ~3-5M (obs,action,mask,mc_return) samples in ~1-2h). scripts/bc_pretrain.py: masked-CE to the scripted action + value regression on mc_return, 3 epochs Adam 3e-4, save bc_init.zip as the G1 initialization. Target after BC: >75% Act-1-only, >40% Act-1-2 at RL start.

PHASE 7 (Self-Imitation + Act-4 state bank): SILCallback maintains a ~50k-transition buffer of the agent's WON episodes and every 4 PPO updates runs advantage-weighted BC (L = -sum max(R-V(s),0)*log pi(a|s), lambda_sil=0.1, active from G4). StateBankCallback deepcopies mgr.run_state on FIRST Act-4 entry (only when HP>40% to avoid banking unwinnable states) into a ring buffer (cap 2000); RichSTS2RunEnv gains an init_state reset option so statebank_frac (0->0.25 across stages) of resets load a sampled Act-4 state, giving dense on-distribution Heart practice without winning 50 floors each time. Refresh the bank as the policy improves.

PHASE 8 (simulator search — the Heart escalation, adopt ONLY if G4/G5 telemetry shows heart_reached high but heart_win flat): build sts2_env/search/combat_mcts.py (PUCT over deepcopy'd CombatState — clone at expansion only; determinized by reseeding CombatState.rng per simulation, averaging ~12 determinizations; priors/leaf-value from the policy/value net) and meta_rollout.py (deepcopy RunManager, K=12 greedy playouts to end-of-act for a win-prob target at draft/shop/relic/map nodes). Use them two ways: (a) offline ExIt — every ~5M steps relabel ~20k policy-visited states with search, add 0.5*MSE(value, search_value) to the value loss and 3 distillation epochs; (b) inference-time — shallow 64-128-sim MCTS on elite/boss/Heart turns ONLY, policy-net argmax elsewhere. This is the single biggest lever toward the final percentages and the only realistic path toward 95%, and it stays fully local.

PHASE 9 (eval + honest reporting): run_eval on RichSTS2RunEnv at A10 full-run, 1000 deterministic episodes (stderr ~1.6% vs the old 200-ep ~3.4%), shaping off, seed block 10_000_000+. Add acts_reached histogram, heart_reached rate, heart_win rate, deaths_by_act, mean_hp_at_each_boss, and a Wilson 95% CI on win_rate to eval_history.jsonl. Report per-act rates, never a single pass/fail gate.

C:\Users\motqu\GitHub\sts2-rl-agent\sts2_env\train\policy.py — replace hand mean-pool with per-slot concat + global-context mean; project the deck bag through the shared card_embedding; add archetype scalars; bump card_embed_dim/hand_hidden to 96; recompute features_dim; update rich_policy_kwargs + transfer_weights
C:\Users\motqu\GitHub\sts2-rl-agent\scripts\heuristic_agent.py — NEW: rule-based Necrobinder policy for BC data-gen

phase1_minimal_changes: train_necrobinder.py: replace the STAGES dict with run-only stages. Minimum viable set to relaunch today: G1 = run env, ascension_level=0, max_act_count=2. Extend StageConfig with ascension_level:int and drop the combat fields' use; make_stage_env builds ONLY RichSTS2RunEnv(character_id='Necrobinder', ascension_level=stage.ascension_level, max_act_count=stage.max_act_count, reward_config=cfg, max_steps=3000). Remove the RichSTS2CombatEnv import/branch.

train_necrobinder.py: kill the frozen optimizer. Delete linear_lr; pass learning_rate=2.0e-4 (constant) and target_kl=0.03 to MaskablePPO in build_model. In the resume path drop custom_objects={'learning_rate': linear_lr}.

train_necrobinder.py: stop the entropy collapse. Delete the progress-tied _on_rollout_start ent anneal; set ent_coef=0.01 constant in build_model. Also set n_steps=1024, gamma=0.997, batch_size=4096, n_epochs=3, and default --n-envs 24.

train_necrobinder.py: stop the silent halt. In _do_eval delete the win-rate shaping anneal block (lines 207-214) and the env_method('set_shaping_scale', ...) push (leave shaping_scale fixed at 1.0). In main() delete the `if not state.get('promoted'): break` (line 440) and pass stop_on_promotion=False so the campaign always trains to budget.

rich_run_env.py: separate truncation from death. Change line 162 `elif truncated: reward += cfg.death` to `elif truncated: reward += 0.0` (add info['truncated']=True). This alone removes the timeout==death confound for the full-run env (the parent's truncation reward is already discarded here).

reward_config.py: add a `truncation: float = 0.0` field and keep win/death/act_completion/floor/combat_hp_retention for now (phase-1 keeps the existing shaping as the 'strong shaping' signal — the low ascension + acts1-2 makes it genuinely winnable, so we can relaunch without the full PBRS rewrite, which is Phase 1 of the full plan). Fix the docstring's shaping_scale-from-winrate note since the anneal is now removed.

LAUNCH COMMAND (from repo root, using the venv): C:\Users\motqu\GitHub\sts2-rl-agent\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts\train_necrobinder.py --stage G1 --total-steps 20000000 --n-envs 24 --eval-episodes 500 --progress   (expect the agent to reach Act 2 and win-rate to climb within the first few M steps; watch eval_history.jsonl for non-zero mean_floors and per-act deaths — the telemetry that was blind before.)
reward_spec: Terminal (never annealed): win=+1.0, death=-1.0, truncation=0.0 (bootstrap, value net treats a slow-but-alive stall differently from a loss), sim_error=0.0 (tag info['sim_error'], never score a simulator bug as death). REMOVE floor (+0.004, pays for entering rooms -> routing mis-incentive), act_completion (+0.25, double-counts progress), combat_hp_retention (0.05*ratio, biases toward over-blocking and AGAINST correct tempo/Osty spends like Bone Shards/Sacrifice), and the entire win-rate-driven shaping_scale anneal (~40% reward jitter per 200-ep eval; zeroes shaping exactly at 80% win rate where the push to 95% happens). REPLACE with a single potential-based term F(s,s') = gamma_shape*Phi(s') - Phi(s), gamma_shape=0.997, added every step, Phi=0 at terminal. Phi(s) = 0.45*progress + 0.30*effective_hp + 0.20*enemy_down, all in [0,1]: progress = (current_act_index + clip(act_floor/17,0,1))/4 (monotone run progress, no take-more-rooms bias because the potential difference is path-independent); effective_hp = clip((current_hp + 0.5*block + 0.3*osty_hp)/max_hp, 0, 1) (run-long HP economy; spending HP/Osty for tempo is only a transient dip refunded when it resolves); enemy_down = 1 - sum(enemy_hp)/sum(enemy_max_hp) in combat, carried at its last value between combats (densifies long fights incl. the Heart; unfarmable — you only gain by actually reducing real enemy HP, and it resets each combat). WHY NON-HACKABLE: PBRS telescopes to gamma^T*Phi_T - Phi_0, a policy-independent constant per episode (Ng-Harada-Russell 1999), so it provably cannot change the optimal policy and no farming loop exists; no term rewards owning 'good' cards, floors entered, or block gained, so there is no proxy to exploit. Deliberately NO hand-authored deck-quality reward — deck competence comes from observability (deck bag) + search value targets, not a hackable per-pick bonus.
curriculum_spec: FULL-RUN ONLY (stages A/B and RichSTS2CombatEnv deleted per constraint). One RichSTS2RunEnv, difficulty controlled on two axes pushed into all envs by a CurriculumController callback each eval that NEVER returns False/halts. Axis 1 = ascension ceiling 0->10 (scales enemy/boss/Heart difficulty while the agent still drafts its OWN deck — the honest replacement for the impossible starter-vs-elite task). Axis 2 = max_act_count 1->4 (RichSTS2RunEnv already supports early-win termination). Stage table (advance on 1000-ep eval gate met x2 OR per-stage budget exhausted — time-based fallback so a stall never halts the ladder; warm-start each from prev best_model): G1 A0 acts1-2 20M gate0.80 statebank0.0; G2 A0 acts1-4 30M gate0.55 statebank0.15 (opens the Heart EARLY so the state bank begins filling and the Heart gets budget across 4 stages, not just the last); G3 A4 acts1-4 40M gate0.50 statebank0.20; G4 A8 acts1-4 50M gate0.45 statebank0.25 SIL-on; G5 A10 acts1-4 60M final statebank0.25. ~200M RL steps total. Per-reset ascension sampler at each ceiling L: {L:0.6, U(0..L):0.3, min(L+2,10):0.1} (anti-forgetting + a little stretch pressure). ACT-4 STATE BANK (the imitation x curriculum synergy for the sparsest, most decisive state): snapshot mgr.run_state on FIRST Act-4 entry (only if HP>40% so we don't bank unwinnable states), ring buffer cap 2000 from the agent's own runs; statebank_frac of resets load a sampled bank state via an init_state option, giving dense on-distribution Heart/Act-4-elite (Spire Shield+Spear, Corrupt Heart) practice without needing a 50-floor win each episode. Promotion is a SPEED knob, never a gate.
architecture_spec: OBSERVATION (rich_observation.py, grows RICH_OBS_SIZE -> from-scratch retrain, acceptable): add a run-level DECK BAG = per-CardId counts of player.deck / BAG_COUNT_SCALE over NUM_CARD_IDS (582), projected through the SAME shared card_embedding table exactly like the combat pile bags at rich_observation.py:336 — this closes the fatal gap where at a CARD_REWARD/SHOP/removal node the policy could not see what it already owned (only 7 aggregate scalars at :499-524). Plus ~8 Necrobinder archetype scalars from the deck (summon-card, Soul-generator, Soul-payoff, Doom-applier, ethereal, OstyAttack, zero-cost counts, upgrade fraction) so archetype coherence (Osty vs Soul/ethereal vs Doom — three disjoint builds competing for the same slots) is learnable. POLICY (policy.py RichFeaturesExtractor.forward): THE key fix — replace hand_pooled = hand_x.mean(dim=1) (policy.py:95, permutation-invariant, destroys per-slot card identity while action i = 'play hand slot i') with a per-slot CONCAT: reshape hand_x (B,10,H) -> (B,10*H) in fixed slot order aligned to the combat action layout, append a mean-pool as global context. Add the deck-bag projection (deck_bag @ card_embedding.weight[1:]) and the 8 archetype scalars to the returned features. Bump card_embed_dim 64->96, hand_hidden 64->96. Torso net_arch stays [1024,1024,512] for pi and vf (trivial for the 4060; GPU was only 35% utilized — the bottleneck was never the torso, it was the permutation-invariant input). STRETCH (only if concat underperforms on multi-target cards): a pointer/attention combat action head keyed on per-slot embeddings for actions 0..114 with a standard linear head for meta actions 115..156, and auxiliary heads (predict end-of-combat HP, turns-to-kill) as representation sharpeners that change no objective and add no reward-hacking surface. Value head: with PBRS + gamma 0.997 (vs the old 0.999 over 3000-step episodes) value-target variance drops sharply, so explained_variance should stay healthy on full runs.
algorithm_and_hparams: Framework: MaskablePPO (sb3-contrib), unchanged, retuned for the long horizon + bootstrapped by BC and refined by SIL/ExIt. PPO: n_envs=24, n_steps=1024 (covers ~1000-2500-step episodes), batch_size=4096, n_epochs=3 (fewer epochs + target_kl reduces value overfit on long episodes), gamma=0.997 (effective horizon ~333; PBRS densifies so the lower gamma cuts variance without losing the terminal signal), gae_lambda=0.95, clip_range=0.2, vf_coef=0.5, max_grad_norm=0.5, device=cuda. LEARNING RATE: constant 2.0e-4 + target_kl=0.03 (PPO self-regulates step size; survives resume, unlike the old linear_lr that decayed to ~1.4e-7 and stayed frozen because progress_remaining resumes near 0). ENTROPY: constant floor ent_coef=0.01 (drop the progress-tied 0.01->0.003 anneal that collapsed entropy to -0.38); optionally cosine 0.02->0.005 on ABSOLUTE timesteps, never tied to progress_remaining. BC (Phase 6): masked-CE to scripted action + MSE to mc_return, Adam 3e-4, 3 epochs over ~3-5M samples, batch 4096 -> bc_init.zip as the G1 init. SIL (Phase 7): 50k won-transition buffer, advantage-weighted BC every 4 updates, lambda_sil=0.1 (cut if entropy_loss drops below ~-0.6). SEARCH (Phase 8): combat MCTS PUCT c_puct=1.5, sims=128 (train/data-gen) / 64-128 (infer), 12 determinizations via CombatState.rng reseed, root Dirichlet alpha=0.3 eps=0.25; meta rollout K=12 to end-of-act; ExIt every 5M steps relabel ~20k states + 0.5*MSE(value, search_value) + 3 distill epochs. Total ~200M RL steps + offline BC/search.
exploration_spec: Layered, in priority order. (1) THE CURRICULUM IS THE PRIMARY EXPLORATION AID: vanilla PPO on A10 almost never reaches Act-3/4/Heart states, so they get zero gradient (the old deep-exploration failure). Starting at A0 with acts open to 4 by G2 means the agent reaches the Heart early and often, so the hardest states are densely visited from the start. (2) THE ACT-4 STATE BANK is directed exploration of the single hardest region — it resets envs straight into Act 4, a region entropy/epsilon-greedy would essentially never reach on-policy. (3) BC-THEN-RL gives a competent, diverse prior (the scripted agent already explores coherent Osty/Soul/Doom lines), so RL explores AROUND competent play rather than from random death at step ~47. (4) SIL replays rare wins so the single terminal reward propagates instead of washing out. (5) FLOORED ENTROPY (ent_coef 0.01 constant) keeps stochasticity on the sparse meta-decisions (card skip, boss relic, path) where the old run collapsed; PBRS helps the known-hard 'correct skip' problem because skipping loses no progress-potential, so a skip is not punished by shaping (unlike a naive floor/deck reward that pushes always-take). (6) SEARCH (Phase 8) is the deepest exploration engine — MCTS visits tactical lines (Osty-HP spends, Doom-detonation timing, Soul-Storm sequencing, Heart card-count minimization under Beat of Death) that shallow policy-gradient never samples. Deliberately NO RND/count-based intrinsic bonus by default — it adds a non-potential reward term and a hacking surface; the diagnosis-preferred route (signal density + imitation + search) is used instead. Only enable a small annealed-to-zero RND in early A0 if the heart_reached eval metric is near 0.
throughput_and_infra: Bottleneck is CPU env-stepping + SB3 subproc IPC, NOT the GPU (35% util; env-only 4442 steps/s vs .learn 1321 at 16 envs). Target ~3-3.5k end-to-end steps/s so 200M steps lands in ~17-20h wall-clock (feasible over ~1 day, resumable). Fixes, cheapest-first: (1) n_envs 16->24 to oversubscribe the 24 threads (~+30-50% env throughput, near-linear to core saturation). (2) CACHE THE ACTION MASK once per state — today it is computed ~2x/step (inside step() and again via action_masks()) and can_play_card runs ~21x/step iterating hooks (~40% of step time); compute once in step(), stash on the env, return from action_masks()/info; memoize can_play_card over (hand,energy,powers) within a turn (~1.5-2x). (3) SHRINK IPC — the obs (~4772 float32 after the deck bag, ~19KB pickled/env/step, ~95% zeros in the bag segments) is >half the .learn overhead; either move to a shared-memory VecEnv (kills per-step pickling — the bigger, cleaner win) or dirty-flag the static obs segments (deck/relic/run-static change rarely) and only re-encode combat segments each step. (4) BC/search data-gen workers play WHOLE games and return batched trajectories — NO per-step IPC — reaching ~50-70k aggregate raw sim-steps/s across the 24 threads; the GPU (idle at 35%) can host batched net inference for the workers for free. Defer C/numba/Cython (the sim is a pure-Python object graph; a hot-path rewrite needs a data-oriented SoA refactor — high effort, low near-term ROI). Re-benchmark after (1)-(3) and re-scale the budget (drop to ~120M) if the target is missed rather than assuming. All inference stays local: compact policy net + optional shallow local MCTS (turn-based game, 50-250ms/move is free).
success_metrics: PRIMARY: A10 Necrobinder full 4-act (Corrupt Heart) win rate, measured on 1000 deterministic eval episodes, shaping off, seed block 10_000_000+, reported with a Wilson 95% CI (stderr ~1.6% at p=0.5, vs the old 200-ep ~3.4% that was too noisy for both the promotion streak-of-2 test and the shaping anneal). SECONDARY / diagnostic (all per-eval, to eval_history.jsonl): acts_reached histogram, heart_reached rate, heart_win rate (Heart is the binding constraint — this is the number that decides whether to escalate to search), deaths_by_act, mean_hp_at_each_boss, mean floors, per-phase reach counts. Per-act win-through targets (honest, not gates): Act1 >90%, Act1-2 >80%, Act1-3 >65%, full+Heart maximize. EVAL PROTOCOL: run at A10 full-run (NOT the training ceiling), 1000 episodes, deterministic, shaping_scale=0; a claim of an improvement must survive the CI (no overlapping CIs from noise). The old eval was diagnostically blind — it used the combat env so info lacked floor/act keys and every line showed mean_floors=0.0, deaths_by_act={'0':N}, hiding the elite/boss ceiling entirely; the run-env eval fixes this because info carries floor/act. Track training-side: explained_variance (should stay >~0.8 with PBRS+gamma 0.997), entropy_loss (must not collapse below ~-0.6), truncation rate (watch for stall-to-avoid-death after the truncation=0 change — cap max_steps ~3000).
risks_and_mitigations: 1. CEILING HONESTY — pure model-free+imitation will NOT reach 95%; realistic ~55-70%. The Corrupt Heart is an anti-synergy wall for exactly this character: Beat of Death 2 punishes Necrobinder's many-0-cost-cards loop, Invincible 200 caps burst, Strength snowballs (+10 on 4th buff, +50 on 5th+), and its winning line is reached only late in near-won runs (sparsest channel). Mitigation: the Act-4 state bank + SIL densify Heart practice; escalate to search (Phase 8) if heart_win stalls — that is the data-driven trigger, not overtraining. 2. DECKBUILDING CREDIT ASSIGNMENT stays long-horizon even with deck observability + PBRS (a pick at floor 3 -> outcome at floor 50); PBRS deliberately does NOT reward deck coherence (to stay non-hackable). Risk: incoherent decks that clear A1-A2 but collapse A3-A4. Mitigation: deck-bag obs + archetype scalars + meta-rollout search value targets (Phase 8) are the real fix; this is the weakest link of a search-free design. 3. PBRS UNDER FUNCTION APPROX is only optimum-invariant in the exact-value limit; a badly-scaled Phi can bias a finite-budget policy. Mitigation: keep Phi tied to legitimate progress/HP/enemy-HP only; ablate Phi terms on G1. 4. TRUNCATION=0 could teach stalling-to-avoid-death. Mitigation: cap max_steps ~3000, monitor truncation rate, treat repeated stalls as terminal-0. 5. OBS/OFFSET + per-slot changes are the riskiest code — a wrong offset silently corrupts training. Mitigation: rich_observation segment tests + a shape/logit smoke test before every long run; the concat fallback keeps the pointer head optional. 6. SCRIPTED-BC BIAS can imprint the heuristic's suboptimal habits. Mitigation: entropy floor + SIL on the agent's OWN wins + BC weight only in P0/early G1. 7. STATE-BANK DISTRIBUTION SHIFT — early banks come from a weak policy (low-HP/unwinnable Act-4 states -> agent learns Heart is hopeless). Mitigation: only bank HP>40% entries, refresh as the policy improves, keep statebank_frac<=0.25. 8. THROUGHPUT SHORTFALL. Mitigation: benchmark early, cut budget to ~120M rather than cutting the ladder. 9. SEARCH COMPLEXITY (Phase 8) — RunManager deepcopy may be far costlier than CombatState's 516us; determinized MCTS can be over-optimistic on the Heart's snowball. Mitigation: cap playout horizon, use fewer determinizations, and keep BC+SIL+state-bank (no ExIt) as the safe fallback that already beats the old run.
honest_outlook: The 95% target at A10 with a Corrupt-Heart finale is above top-human full-run win rates and should be treated as aspirational, not a pass/fail gate — the old ladder proved that gating on an unreachable threshold silently kills the whole campaign. Realistic, defensible outcomes: the model-free full-run spine + PBRS + architecture/optimizer fixes alone should reach a strong, honest base — Act1 ~90%+, Act1-2 ~80%, Act1-3 ~65%, full-run-to-Heart ~55-65%, with the Heart as the binding constraint (heart_reached high, heart_win the residual loss). Adding the BC bootstrap, SIL, and the Act-4 state bank pushes the full-run number toward the upper end of that band (~65-70%) more sample-efficiently and de-risks the cold start. The ONLY realistic path toward the 80-90%+ regime — and the only lever with a plausible (still uncertain) line to ~95% — is exploiting the exact, resettable simulator with search: offline MCTS/ExIt to densify deckbuilding credit and, decisively, shallow local inference-time MCTS gated to elite/boss/Heart turns (fully local, latency-free on a turn-based game). Even then, irreducible variance from card/relic/map RNG and unwinnable hands, plus the Heart's structural anti-synergy against Necrobinder, likely cap a fully-local policy somewhere in the ~70-85% range rather than 95%. The right posture: build the measurable base fast, let the per-act/heart_win telemetry decide when and where to spend search, and report the achieved per-act rates with confidence intervals honestly rather than chasing a single headline number the game's design makes near-unreachable.
rationale: Each of the three proposals is individually incomplete for this problem: Design A (pure PPO) is honest and relaunchable but its own authors cap it at ~60-72% because it has no answer to the Heart's sparsity or deckbuilding credit assignment; Design B (search/ExIt) has the strongest tools but its highest-risk, highest-effort infrastructure (custom pointer policy, MCTS, RunManager deepcopy, ExIt loop) is the wrong thing to gate a same-day relaunch on and would repeat the old failure of spending days before seeing a signal; Design C (heuristic-BC + SIL + state-bank) has the best cheap accelerators but, alone, still tops out near 55-70%. The synthesis sequences them by risk-adjusted ROI: ship A's spine first (it is the minimum that makes the FIRST full-run gradient happen and turns on the per-act telemetry that was blind before), then layer C's cheap accelerators (BC/SIL/state-bank are low-code and directly attack the sparse-terminal long horizon and the Heart), and reserve B's expensive search for exactly the place telemetry will prove it is needed (heart_win) and for the fully-local inference-time planning that is the only credible route toward the aspirational target. Every hard constraint is honored: full-run-only training (A/B stages and the combat env deleted), fully-local inference (compact net + optional shallow local MCTS on a turn-based game), and the 4060 + 24-thread budget (throughput fixes make 200M steps ~1 day; the GPU was never the bottleneck). The reward is provably non-hackable (PBRS), the optimizer stays alive, the policy can finally read its cards and deck, and the campaign can never silently halt — the five root causes of the original failure are each structurally closed.
